pages.text: Three things let you rest in Novus, and they are not interchangeable. A $(bold)bed$() is the one that matters most — it is still the only thing that moves your spawn point. $(italic)Comforts$() adds two companions for resting on the move: the $(bold)sleeping bag$() and the $(bold)hammock$().
pages.title: Rest & the Long Night

pages.text: $(bold)A bed$() behaves exactly as you remember: sleep through the night, wake at dawn, and set your spawn where it stands. It is the canonical reset point — the place the world returns you to.$(br2)Keep one at your main base and sleep in it once, so the world knows where home is. Vanilla rules still hold: never place a bed in the Nether or the End.
pages.title: The Bed

pages.text: $(bold)A sleeping bag$() is the traveller's bed. Place it and you climb in at once — no second click. It skips the night like a bed, works in any biome, and $(bold)leaves your spawn point untouched$(). It never wears out, either: break it after waking and carry it to the next camp.$(br2)One limit — a sleeping bag can only be used at $(bold)night$(). It is crafted from wool; check EMI for the recipe.
pages.title: Sleeping Bags

pages.text: $(bold)A hammock$() is the mirror image. It can only be used by $(bold)day$(), and it sleeps you through to nightfall. If you keep odd hours — mining through the daylight, working the surface after dark — a hammock is how you turn the clock around.$(br2)Like the sleeping bag, it never touches your spawn point.
pages.title: Hammocks

pages.text: $(bold)Both count against phantoms.$() Resting in a sleeping bag or a hammock resets the restless-nights counter and stops phantoms from spawning, exactly as a full night's sleep in a bed would.$(br2)A short hammock nap in broad daylight is enough to keep the sky clear — you are never forced into a bed just to be rid of them.
pages.title: Phantoms
